name: Enterprise ICP & Account Segmentation
description: Defining the top 100 target accounts using a rigorous 3-tier segmentation model based on strategic value, workforce size, and compliance risk exposure. Enterprise ABM does not treat all accounts equally — resources are allocated based on tier.
details: Tier 1 (Top 15 "Whales"): Revenue >$1B — Lendlease, CIMIC, John Holland, Multiplex, Downer
Tier 2 (Next 35): Revenue $200M–$1B — Built, Fulton Hogan, BMD, BESIX/Watpac, Kane
Tier 3 (Remaining 50): Large regional builders and construction firms
Focus: Enterprise builders only — architecture firms, certifiers, and industry bodies are a separate future strategy

name: Buying Committee Persona Framework
description: Enterprise deals involve 6–11 stakeholders across the buying committee. Tools™ must map and tailor messaging to each persona within every target account to multi-thread effectively.
details: Quality/Compliance Director (Champion): Standardises compliance, reduces defect rates
Construction/Project Manager (End User): Saves time interpreting codes, mobile-first
COO (Economic Buyer): Reduces rework costs, mitigates legal/insurance risk
Training/Development Manager (Influencer): Accelerates onboarding, interactive CPD
IT/Systems Director (Technical Evaluator): ISO 9001 certified, data sovereignty, Revit integration

name: Account Planning & Intelligence
description: Creating detailed account dossiers for Tier 1 and Tier 2 targets, including recent company news, major project wins, identified stakeholders, and hypotheses on their specific compliance pain points.
details: Weeks 1 to 4 — before any outreach occurs
Involves cleaning CRM data and ensuring tracking mechanisms are in place
Align sales and marketing on the finalised Target Account List (TAL)
Create dossiers with recent news, project wins, and pain point hypotheses

name: Mining Existing Database
description: Identifying current free users or Individual PRO users employed by target Tier 1 and Tier 2 firms to serve as internal champions and first-party intent signals.
targetAudience: Existing Free Users at Target Firms
details: Audit current free users to identify enterprise accounts with organic adoption
Define creative landing pages and establish key CTAs
Implement self-qualification mechanisms
Flag PLG expansion opportunities for Engage phase

name: External Data Enrichment
description: ACTIVATE LATER: Once the internal known-contacts pathway is mastered, employ external data enrichment tools to identify Quality Directors, COOs, and IT Directors not yet in the CRM, completing the buying committee map at each target account.
details: Delay outreach until the target list is fully created
Complete buying committee map for all Tier 1 and Tier 2 accounts
Identify 6–10 key stakeholders at each account
Use tools like ZoomInfo, Apollo, or LinkedIn Sales Navigator

name: High-Value Direct Mail & Executive Gifting
description: Physical pattern interrupts to command executive attention. In an era of overflowing digital inboxes, direct mail serves as a powerful differentiator that becomes a topic of conversation within the target account.
details: "Site Safety Audit" Kit: custom-branded hard hat with prospect's logo + Tools™ logo
Bespoke printed report on their projects + digitised compliance savings
Dimensional mailers (boxes, tubes) — rarely intercepted by gatekeepers
Handwritten note from the Tools™ founder + Personalised URL (PURL)
Glossy brochures with QR codes ("10 Enterprise Playbooks")

name: Omni-channel Content Distribution
description: Flooding the market with high-value thought leadership content across multiple channels, focusing on the cost of defects, NCC complexity, and digital compliance transformation.
details: LinkedIn posts on the company page
PR releases and blog republishing
Content syndication: whitepapers and case studies through MBA, HIA publications
Thought leadership: cost of building defects, NCC update complexities

name: Targeted Advertising & Competitor Conquesting
description: Running targeted ads to buying committee personas within the top 100 accounts, including competitor conquesting ads to capture high-intent accounts researching alternatives.
details: Primary channel: LinkedIn — precise account and persona targeting not replicable on Meta for enterprise
Google Search: competitor conquesting ads — "Tools vs. Hammertech", "Procore Alternatives"
Google Display Remarketing: cookie ABM landing page visitors and remarket across display network (news, industry sites)
Meta excluded from enterprise plan — broader awareness only, separate future initiative
Focus on thought leadership: cost of defects, NCC complexity, digital compliance transformation

name: 3-Part Short Sequence
description: Deploying a concise, impactful email sequence to the curated target list designed to generate initial awareness and interest before the full engagement cadence begins.
details: 3-part email sequence
Concise, impactful messaging with account-specific relevance
Designed for initial awareness — not the full ABM cadence
Timed to land after content warm-up phase (Week 5+)

name: Product-Led Growth Escalation
description: Leveraging existing free-tier usage within enterprise accounts as the ultimate first-party intent data source. When site managers at a Tier 1 firm begin using the free version, this signals a massive enterprise opportunity.
details: Executive Escalation: "Hi [CIO], three of your site teams are using Tools™. Can I show you the executive dashboard?"
Create confidential internal case studies using actual product usage data
Show how one division was successful to sell enterprise-wide licence to procurement/IT
Upsell path: Free → Individual PRO → Enterprise

name: Video Prospecting
description: Sales reps recording short, personalised 60-second videos showing the prospect's actual website or a mock-up of their project inside the Tools™ interface. This pattern interrupt is highly effective for executive outreach.
targetAudience: Key Decision Makers at Target Accounts
details: 60-second personalised demo videos per account
Show prospect's actual project inside Tools™ (e.g., Box Gutter compliance)
Use tools like Vidyard or Loom — video emails lift open rates 35%+
Click-through rates increase by over 150% with embedded video

name: VIP Experiences & FCON-Tech Strategy
description: Orchestrating targeted micro-events and VIP experiences around major industry gatherings. Face-to-face trust is paramount in enterprise construction — events condense the sales cycle by creating high-touch interactions.
details: Tier 1 VIP Dinners: Intimate, invite-only (8-12 people), CEO-hosted, peer networking focus
Digital CPD Summit: Short 45-min sessions (3 presenters), educational format with sales undertone — low cost, perfect for C-Suite and middle management
Exclusive virtual roundtables hosted by Jerry Tyrrell on NCC compliance and quality management
FCON-Tech 2026 (Sydney, Oct): Pre-event outreach 2 weeks prior, orchestrate 1:1 coffee meetings
During FCON-Tech: Deploy executives, host private breakfast roundtable near venue
Post-event: Follow up within 24 hours with conversation-specific messaging
Cost lens: no booths — identify target attendees in advance and orchestrate in-person touchpoints

name: Layered Targeting & Social Selling
description: Moving beyond job titles to target by function and seniority with a hyper-personalised warm-up sequence. Upload the verified top 100 account list directly into LinkedIn Campaign Manager for precision targeting.
details: Competitor conquesting ads: comparison-focused (Tools™ vs. Hammertech)
Warm-up sequence: Engage content → Connect → Follow up with value, not pitches
Day +1/+2/+5 post-connection with high-value assets (ROI calculator)
Layer targeting: C-Suite (ROI), Operations/HSE (compliance), IT/Procurement (security)

name: Intent-Driven Email Cadence
description: Deliberately multi-threading to three or more roles across the buying committee simultaneously. True ABM email requires account-specific relevance in the very first line — if a template can be sent to 500 people without alteration, it is not ABM.
details: Day 1 (Hook): Specific account trigger — e.g., recent project win, compliance news
Day 4 (Proof): Embedded personalised video via Vidyard/Loom
Day 7 (Value): Relevant case study — Hutchinson Builders or Richard Crookes
Day 12 (Breakup): Polite withdrawal, leaves door open

name: 1:1 and 1:Few Web Experiences
description: Custom-built landing pages for each target account. Generic homepages do not convert enterprise buyers — they should land on a page built specifically for them, featuring their logo, tailored messaging, and relevant case studies.
details: 1:1 pages built per account: "How Hickory Can Digitise WHS Compliance Across Active Sites"
Feature prospect's logo, pre-filled ROI calculator, direct calendar link to AE
1:Few pages for verticals: Civil Engineering vs. Commercial High-Rise
Serve case studies and compliance features specific to each sub-sector
exampleLabel: View Example: Hickory ABM Landing Page

name: Initial Sales Process
description: Deep-diving into compliance challenges and presenting the tailored Tools™ solution to qualified prospects, supported by the full arsenal of enterprise sales collateral.
details: Supported by Enterprise Pitch Decks
Implementation guides and use cases
Custom pricing models
Leverage visual product demonstrations as the "aha moment"

name: Weaponising Existing Assets
description: Tools™ has unique competitive advantages that must be systematically deployed across all ABM touchpoints — endorsements from Tier 1 clients, certifications, patents, and the CPD partnership.
details: Hutchinson Builders & Richard Crookes endorsements — strongest social proof available
Visual product demos: "A picture tells 1,000 words" — show complex NCC simplified into one graphic
ISO 9001 Certification & Patents: enterprise-grade credibility for risk-averse procurement
CPD/PointsBuild partnership: enterprise firms mandated to provide CPD, Tools™ solves this

name: Customised Proposals & Enablement
description: Once an opportunity is created, marketing supports sales to accelerate the deal with tailored business cases and enablement resources for the internal champion.
details: Develop tailored business cases / ROI models for the specific account
Stakeholder enablement: security docs for IT, training overviews for HR
Provide champion with resources to sell Tools™ to the rest of the buying committee
Facilitate executive alignment: peer-to-peer meetings between leadership teams

name: Final Sales Process
description: Presenting the formal proposal and securing the enterprise contract, leveraging the full weight of Tier 1 endorsements and proven ROI.
details: Supported by Proposal Decks
Standardised contracts
Relevant success stories: Hutchinson Builders, Richard Crookes references
Tier 1 goal: secure pilot programs on major flagship projects

name: Routing & Immediate Value
description: Handling outcomes based on self-qualification, routing prospects to the appropriate conversion path with immediate value delivery.
details: Small Teams → "Buy Now" self-serve
Enterprise Teams → "Book A Demo"
Receive immediate value: 10 Enterprise Playbooks
Qualify for 30 Days Complimentary PRO Access
30-Day Quick Win pathway: structured onboarding milestones to achieve measurable value fast — change inertia (not price) is the #1 adoption barrier
First 7 days: one team, one tool type, first compliance query answered. First 30 days: first site audit completed digitally

name: Account-Centric Metrics Framework
description: Traditional lead-gen metrics (cost per lead, total traffic) are insufficient for enterprise ABM. Success is measured using account-centric metrics that track engagement depth, pipeline quality, and deal velocity.
details: Account Engagement: % of target accounts engaged, stakeholders engaged per account
Pipeline Generation: SQOs from top 100, total pipeline value from target accounts
Deal Velocity: Average time from first engagement to closed-won (ABM should shorten this)
Revenue & ROI: Enterprise ARR generated, expansion revenue, overall ABM program ROI
